article_meta.title: Marchers protesting planned deportations block major freeway in Los Angeles 1 of 18 | Thousands of people protesting mas

reasoning: The model returned an empty array for what is essentially a photo gallery/caption article from the Associated Press with very minimal author prose. The actual article text is extremely brief: 'Thousands of people protesting mass deportations planned by President Donald Trump marched in Southern California on Sunday. Demonstrators in downtown Los Angeles blocked a major freeway. Protests were also held in Dallas and McAllen, Texas.' The rest is photo captions. This is relatively neutral AP wire copy. However, there are some arguable bias instances: the term 'mass deportations' in the author's prose could be seen as loaded word choice (characterizing planned deportations as 'mass' without sourcing that characterization), and there's a potential Bias by Omission in that only the protesters' perspective is presented with no government/administration rationale included. The article is so short though that these are debatable. The empty array is defensible but misses at least one arguable instance.

custom_reasoning.attribution_rule_compliance: No attribution violations since nothing was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_positive_rate: No false positives since nothing was flagged.
custom_reasoning.false_negative_rate: The article is very brief AP wire copy, but 'mass deportations' is arguably loaded Word Choice in the author's own prose - the author characterizes planned deportations as 'mass' without attribution. There's also a potential Bias by Omission - only the protest perspective is presented, no government rationale for the deportation policy. These are debatable given the brevity of the piece, but at least one instance could have been flagged.
custom_reasoning.bias_type_accuracy: No labels to evaluate since nothing was flagged.
